definition: to become less intense or widespread
example: The storm began to abate after midnight.

definition: to make something better
example: New policies helped ameliorate the housing shortage.

definition: having mixed feelings about something
example: She felt ambivalent about moving to a new city.

definition: something that deviates from what is standard or expected
example: The sudden spike in traffic was an anomaly.

definition: very difficult and requiring a lot of effort
example: Training for the marathon was an arduous process.

definition: shortness of duration or expression
example: For brevity, he summarized the report in three bullet points.

definition: truthful and straightforward; frank
example: He gave a candid assessment of the project’s risks.

definition: to come together to form one whole
example: Over time, the small teams coalesced into a single department.

definition: to agree
example: I concur with your conclusion.

definition: easily seen or noticed; attracting attention
example: Her bright jacket made her conspicuous in the crowd.

definition: showing careful and persistent work or effort
example: He was diligent about reviewing his notes every day.

definition: to draw out a response or reaction
example: The question elicited a surprising answer.

definition: lasting for a very short time
example: The beauty of the sunset was ephemeral.

definition: to encourage or promote the development of something
example: Good leaders foster a culture of trust.

definition: careful about spending money; economical
example: She’s frugal, but never stingy.

definition: flawless; without errors
example: His timing was impeccable.

definition: too great or extreme to be expressed in words
example: They described the view as ineffable.

definition: clear and easy to understand
example: Her explanation was lucid and persuasive.

definition: showing great attention to detail; very careful
example: He kept meticulous records of every expense.

definition: present, appearing, or found everywhere
example: Smartphones are ubiquitous in modern life.
